metadata.narrative_en: BLS-1 and BLS-3 become usable, while BLS-2 remains channelized and BLS-4 stays limited as a heavy exit.
metadata.adjudication_method: Deterministic operational matrix using BLS terrain score, throughput, EW, artillery/USV shock, mobility friction, Blue reserve timing, and phase-line progress.

terrain_effects.limitations: No local bathymetry, beach gradient, surf, road network, or elevation layer was available.

properties.name: BLS-1
properties.mobility_summary: Limited but usable mobility; supports dispersion and deception.
properties.selection_rationale: Western fixing site with better water separation than nearby alternatives.

properties.name: BLS-2
properties.mobility_summary: Moderate mobility; wetland/sabkha proximity channels vehicles.
properties.selection_rationale: Useful supporting site with acceptable spacing, but inland wetland proximity constrains expansion.

properties.name: BLS-3
properties.mobility_summary: Good beach exit; defender proximity makes suppression and tempo critical.
properties.selection_rationale: Best main-effort score inside the central frontage; dry/open belt and broadest exit potential.

properties.name: BLS-4
properties.mobility_summary: Urban/industrial/water friction; suitable for fixing/envelopment, not heavy follow-on throughput.
properties.selection_rationale: Selected for frontage and eastern pressure despite low terrain score; treated as limited, not a clean throughput site.

properties.adjudication_note: Aggregate formation marker; losses are company-equivalent at scenario level.
